title: teucrium polium subsp. thymoides
intro: Teucrium polium subsp. thymoides, also known as thyme-leaved germander, is a subspecies of the germander plant (Teucrium polium) that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of thyme-leaved germander include its ability to aid digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and support respiratory health. Thyme-leaved germander can be prepared in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
